instructions: Pound chicken to even thickness, season, coat in flour, beaten egg, then panko.
Deep fry at 170C for 5-6 minutes until golden and cooked through; drain and rest.
Sauté sliced onion, carrot, and potato in a pot for 5 minutes.
Add water, bring to a boil, simmer until vegetables are tender, then add curry roux; stir until thickened.
Slice the katsu, serve over rice with curry sauce poured alongside.
tips: Press the panko firmly onto the chicken for a thick, even crust.
Let curry roux dissolve off the heat for a smoother sauce.

name: Edamame
description: Steamed young soybeans in the pod, sprinkled with sea salt.
instructions: Bring water to a boil with a pinch of salt.
Add frozen edamame and cook for 3-4 minutes until tender.
Drain and toss with sea salt.
Serve warm or at room temperature as an appetizer.
tips: Squeeze the pods to pop the beans directly into your mouth.
A sprinkle of chili flakes or garlic salt adds variety.

name: Bibimbap
description: Korean mixed rice bowl with seasoned vegetables, egg, beef, and gochujang sauce.
instructions: Marinate sliced beef in soy sauce, garlic, and sesame oil for 15 minutes; stir-fry until cooked.
Blanch spinach and bean sprouts separately; season each with sesame oil and salt.
Julienne carrot and zucchini; sauté each separately until just tender.
Place rice in bowls, arrange all toppings in sections on top, fry an egg sunny-side up for each bowl.
Serve with gochujang on the side; mix everything together before eating.
tips: Each vegetable should be cooked and seasoned separately for distinct flavors.
For dolsot bibimbap, use a hot stone bowl to get crispy rice at the bottom.

name: Kimchi Fried Rice
description: Spicy Korean fried rice made with aged kimchi, gochujang, and topped with a fried egg.
instructions: Roughly chop kimchi, reserving the juice.
Heat oil in a wok, stir-fry kimchi with its juice for 2-3 minutes.
Add cold rice, gochujang, and soy sauce; toss on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Fry eggs sunny-side up in a separate pan.
Plate rice, top with fried egg, sprinkle sesame seeds and spring onion; break yolk before eating.
tips: Aged, sour kimchi works best — fresh kimchi lacks the depth of flavor.
The runny egg yolk mixed into the rice creates a rich, creamy sauce.

name: Korean BBQ Beef (Bulgogi)
description: Thinly sliced beef marinated in a sweet soy-pear sauce, grilled or pan-fried.
instructions: Freeze beef for 30 minutes for easier slicing, then slice paper-thin against the grain.
Blend pear, soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger into a marinade.
Marinate beef for at least 30 minutes, or overnight for best results.
Cook on a hot grill or pan in batches over high heat for 1-2 minutes per side.
Garnish with sesame seeds and spring onion; serve with rice and lettuce wraps.
tips: Pear is a natural meat tenderizer and adds subtle sweetness.
Do not overcrowd the pan — cook in a single layer for proper searing.

name: Japchae
description: Korean stir-fried sweet potato glass noodles with vegetables and sesame.
instructions: Cook sweet potato noodles in boiling water for 6-7 minutes until chewy; drain and cut shorter with scissors.
Blanch spinach, squeeze dry, season with sesame oil and salt.
Stir-fry each vegetable separately in a little oil until just tender.
Toss noodles with soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil, and garlic.
Combine everything in a large bowl, add sesame seeds, and serve warm or at room temperature.
tips: Cooking each vegetable separately preserves their individual textures.
These noodles are chewy by nature — do not overcook them.

name: Kimchi
description: Korean fermented napa cabbage with gochugaru, garlic, ginger, and fish sauce.
instructions: Quarter cabbage, salt generously between leaves, and let sit 2 hours; rinse and drain.
Blend garlic, ginger, fish sauce, sugar, and a splash of water into a paste; mix with gochugaru.
Julienne daikon and cut spring onion into 2-inch pieces.
Rub the paste thoroughly between each cabbage leaf, adding daikon and spring onion.
Pack tightly into a jar, leave at room temperature for 1-3 days, then refrigerate.
tips: The cabbage should be wilted but still crunchy after salting.
Ferment longer in cool weather, shorter in warm weather; taste daily.

name: Pad Thai
description: Thailand's iconic stir-fried rice noodles with tamarind sauce, shrimp, peanuts, and lime.
instructions: Soak rice noodles in warm water for 20 minutes until pliable; drain.
Mix tamarind paste, fish sauce, and sugar into the pad thai sauce.
Heat oil in a wok, fry cubed tofu until golden, then add shrimp and cook until pink.
Push everything to one side, scramble eggs on the other, then add noodles and sauce; toss vigorously.
Add bean sprouts and chives in the last 30 seconds; serve with crushed peanuts, lime wedge, and chili flakes.
tips: Soak noodles, do not boil them — they finish cooking in the wok.
The wok must be very hot for authentic pad thai flavor.

name: Thai Green Curry
description: Aromatic coconut-based curry with green chili paste, Thai basil, and vegetables.
instructions: Heat oil, fry green curry paste for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the thick cream from the top of the coconut milk; stir-fry with the paste for 2 minutes.
Add chicken or tofu, cook for 3-4 minutes until sealed.
Pour in remaining coconut milk, add eggplant, bamboo shoots, kaffir lime leaves, fish sauce, and sugar; simmer 10 minutes.
Stir in Thai basil, top with sliced red chili, and serve with jasmine rice.
tips: Frying the curry paste in coconut cream first releases its full aroma.
Fish sauce, not salt, is the primary seasoning in Thai curries.

name: Tom Yum Soup
description: Spicy and sour Thai soup with shrimp, lemongrass, galangal, and lime.
instructions: Bruise lemongrass stalks, slice galangal and tear lime leaves.
Bring broth to a boil with lemongrass, galangal, lime leaves, and crushed chilies.
Add halved mushrooms and tomatoes; simmer 5 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook until pink, about 2-3 minutes.
Turn off heat, add fish sauce, lime juice, and sugar; garnish with coriander.
tips: Add lime juice off the heat to preserve its bright flavor.
The aromatics (lemongrass, galangal, lime leaves) are for flavor — not meant to be eaten.

name: Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Krapao)
description: Quick stir-fry of minced chicken with holy basil, chili, and garlic over rice.
instructions: Pound garlic and chilies in a mortar into a rough paste.
Heat oil in a wok until smoking, add the garlic-chili paste and stir-fry 30 seconds.
Add minced chicken, break up, and cook on high heat until browned.
Season with soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce, and sugar; toss well.
Stir in basil leaves until just wilted; serve over rice with a crispy fried egg on top.
tips: Holy basil is traditional — it has a peppery, clove-like flavor different from sweet basil.
The wok must be screaming hot for authentic Thai street food flavor.

name: Massaman Curry
description: Rich Thai curry with tender beef, potatoes, peanuts, and warm spices in coconut milk.
instructions: Cut beef into cubes, quarter potatoes and onion.
Heat thick coconut cream in a pot, fry curry paste for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add beef and sear in the paste for 3-4 minutes.
Pour in remaining coconut milk, add potatoes, onion, cinnamon, and bay leaves; simmer for 40-50 minutes until beef is tender.
Season with fish sauce, tamarind, palm sugar, and peanuts; serve with jasmine rice.
tips: Massaman is the mildest Thai curry — perfect for those who prefer less heat.
The curry improves significantly the next day as flavors meld.

name: Pho
description: Vietnamese aromatic beef broth noodle soup with rare beef, herbs, and bean sprouts.
instructions: Char onion and ginger under a broiler until blackened; this adds smoky depth.
Simmer broth with charred onion, ginger, star anise, cinnamon, cloves, and fish sauce for 45 minutes; strain.
Cook rice noodles according to package; place in bowls.
Arrange raw sliced beef on top of the noodles.
Pour piping hot broth over the beef (it cooks instantly); serve with bean sprouts, basil, lime, hoisin, and sriracha.
tips: Slice the beef paper-thin — freezing it for 20 minutes makes this easier.
The broth must be boiling hot to cook the raw beef when poured over.

name: Banh Mi
description: Vietnamese crusty baguette sandwich with pate, pickled vegetables, and fresh herbs.
instructions: Julienne daikon and carrot, pickle in rice vinegar, sugar, and salt for 15 minutes.
Slice baguettes lengthwise, toast lightly until crispy outside but soft inside.
Spread pate on one side and mayonnaise on the other.
Layer sliced meat, pickled vegetables, cucumber slices, cilantro, and sliced jalapeno.
Press closed and serve immediately.
tips: The baguette should be crusty but light — Vietnamese bakeries make a distinctive airy bread.
The contrast of rich pate, tangy pickles, and fresh herbs is what makes banh mi special.

name: Vietnamese Spring Rolls
description: Fresh rice paper rolls filled with shrimp, vermicelli, herbs, and vegetables.
instructions: Cook rice vermicelli, drain, and rinse with cold water.
Julienne cucumber and carrot, halve the shrimp lengthwise.
Dip rice paper in warm water for 10 seconds until pliable.
Layer lettuce, vermicelli, vegetables, herbs, and shrimp on the wrapper; fold sides in and roll tightly.
Serve with peanut dipping sauce (peanut butter, hoisin, sriracha, lime juice, and warm water).
tips: Do not over-soak the rice paper — it should be slightly stiff and will continue softening.
Roll tightly for a neat presentation; practice makes perfect.

name: Bun Cha
description: Vietnamese grilled pork meatballs and sliced pork belly with rice noodles and dipping broth.
instructions: Mix ground pork with 1 tbsp fish sauce, garlic, shallot, and 1 tsp sugar; shape into small meatballs.
Marinate pork belly slices in fish sauce, sugar, and garlic.
Grill meatballs and pork belly over high heat until charred and cooked through.
Make dipping broth: warm water, fish sauce, sugar, rice vinegar, and sliced chili.
Serve grilled meats in the dipping broth alongside dry rice noodles, lettuce, herbs, and pickled carrot.
tips: The dipping broth should be sweet, sour, and salty in balance.
Charring the meat over flame is essential for the smoky flavor.

name: Stir-Fried Tofu with Vegetables
description: Crispy pan-fried tofu tossed with mixed vegetables in a savory garlic-soy sauce.
instructions: Press tofu for 15 minutes to remove moisture, cut into cubes, and toss with cornstarch.
Pan-fry tofu in oil until golden and crispy on all sides; remove and set aside.
In the same wok, stir-fry broccoli, bell pepper, and snap peas for 3 minutes.
Add garlic, soy sauce, and oyster sauce; cook 1 minute.
Return tofu, toss with sesame oil, and serve over rice.
tips: Pressing the tofu is essential — wet tofu will not crisp up.
The cornstarch coating gives tofu a satisfying crunch.

name: Falafel
description: Crispy deep-fried chickpea fritters seasoned with herbs, cumin, and coriander.
instructions: Drain soaked chickpeas (do not use canned) and pulse in a food processor with onion, garlic, and herbs until coarse but holds together.
Mix in cumin, coriander, salt, pepper, and baking powder.
Refrigerate mixture for 30 minutes, then shape into small patties or balls.
Deep fry in 180C oil for 3-4 minutes until deep golden and crispy.
Serve in pita with tahini sauce, pickles, and fresh salad.
tips: Never use canned chickpeas — they are too wet and falafel will fall apart.
Soaking dried chickpeas overnight is essential for the right texture.

name: Chicken Shawarma
description: Warmly spiced chicken thighs roasted with cumin, paprika, and turmeric, sliced thin.
instructions: Combine yogurt, all spices, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, and salt into a marinade.
Coat chicken thighs thoroughly, marinate for at least 1 hour (overnight is best).
Bake at 220C for 20-25 minutes, or grill until charred on edges and cooked through.
Let rest 5 minutes, then slice thin against the grain.
Serve in warm pita with pickled turnips, tahini sauce, and fresh salad.
tips: Chicken thighs stay juicier than breast for shawarma.
Broiling for the last 2-3 minutes gives the signature charred edges.

name: Tabbouleh
description: Fresh Lebanese salad of finely chopped parsley, bulgur wheat, tomato, and lemon.
instructions: Soak bulgur in hot water for 15 minutes until soft; drain and squeeze out excess water.
Finely chop parsley, mint, tomatoes, and spring onion.
Combine bulgur with the chopped herbs and vegetables.
Dress with lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper; toss well.
Serve at room temperature — tabbouleh should be predominantly green with parsley.
tips: Parsley is the star, not the bulgur — use far more parsley than grain.
Squeeze excess water from the bulgur to prevent a soggy salad.

name: Baba Ganoush
description: Smoky roasted eggplant dip blended with tahini, lemon, and garlic.
instructions: Roast whole eggplants directly over a gas flame or under a broiler until skin is charred and flesh is collapsed.
Peel off charred skin, drain the flesh for 10 minutes to remove excess liquid.
Mash or pulse eggplant flesh with tahini, lemon juice, garlic, and salt.
Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle paprika and parsley.
Serve with warm pita bread or raw vegetables.
tips: Charring over an open flame gives the signature smoky flavor.
Draining the eggplant prevents a watery dip.

name: Middle Eastern Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a rich tomato and bell pepper sauce seasoned with cumin and harissa.
instructions: Heat olive oil, sauté diced onion and bell pepper until soft.
Add garlic, cumin, paprika, and harissa; cook for 1 minute.
Add canned tomatoes, season with salt, and simmer for 10 minutes until thick.
Make wells, crack eggs into the sauce, cover, and cook 5-7 minutes until whites are set.
Crumble feta over top, garnish with parsley, serve with crusty bread.
tips: The sauce should be thick before adding eggs — too thin and eggs sink to the bottom.
Cover the pan to cook egg whites from the top as well.

name: Lamb Kofta
description: Spiced ground lamb shaped onto skewers and grilled until charred and juicy.
instructions: Grate onion, squeeze out excess moisture.
Mix lamb with grated onion, garlic, parsley, all spices, salt, and pepper; knead for 2 minutes.
Shape mixture onto flat skewers in long oval shapes.
Grill over high heat for 3-4 minutes per side until charred but still juicy inside.
Serve with pita, hummus, tahini sauce, and fresh salad.
tips: Kneading the meat mixture well binds it so it holds on the skewer.
Squeezing moisture from the onion prevents the kofta from falling apart.

name: Fattoush
description: Levantine bread salad with crispy pita chips, vegetables, and a tangy sumac dressing.
instructions: Tear or cut pita into pieces, toast or fry until crispy and golden.
Chop lettuce, cucumber, tomatoes, radishes, spring onion, mint, and parsley.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, sumac, pomegranate molasses, and salt into a dressing.
Toss vegetables with dressing, then add crispy pita just before serving.
Serve immediately — the pita should be crunchy, not soggy.
tips: Add pita at the very last moment to maintain crunch.
Sumac provides the signature tangy, lemony flavor.

name: Mujaddara
description: Middle Eastern comfort dish of lentils and rice topped with crispy caramelized onions.
instructions: Cook lentils in water for 15 minutes until half-tender.
Add washed rice, cumin, cinnamon, and salt to the lentils; cook on low heat for 15 minutes until both are tender.
While rice cooks, thinly slice all onions and fry in olive oil on medium-low heat for 20-25 minutes until deeply caramelized and crispy.
Fluff the rice and lentils with a fork.
Serve topped generously with crispy fried onions and a side of yogurt.
tips: The crispy onions are the soul of this dish — do not rush them.
Cook onions on medium-low heat for deep caramelization, not burning.

name: Labneh
description: Thick strained yogurt served as a creamy dip with olive oil and za'atar.
instructions: Mix yogurt with salt.
Place in a cheesecloth-lined strainer over a bowl; refrigerate for 12-24 hours to drain whey.
Transfer thick labneh to a plate, spread into a swirl.
Drizzle generously with olive oil, sprinkle za'atar and mint.
Serve with warm pita bread or fresh vegetables.
tips: The longer you strain, the thicker the labneh — 24 hours gives a cream cheese consistency.
Full-fat yogurt is essential; low-fat versions produce a grainy texture.

name: Lamb Shawarma Wrap
description: Thin-sliced spiced lamb wrapped in flatbread with pickles, tahini, and fresh vegetables.
instructions: Slice lamb thin, marinate in yogurt, all spices, garlic, and salt for at least 2 hours.
Cook lamb in a hot pan or grill in batches until charred and cooked through.
Warm flatbreads on a hot pan for 30 seconds each side.
Layer lamb, lettuce, sliced tomato, and pickled turnips on the flatbread.
Drizzle with tahini, roll tightly, and serve.
tips: Marinating overnight yields the most flavorful shawarma.
Cook lamb in small batches on very high heat for proper charring.

name: Muhammara
description: Syrian roasted red pepper and walnut dip with pomegranate molasses and cumin.
instructions: Toast walnuts in a dry pan for 3-4 minutes until fragrant.
Pulse roasted red peppers, walnuts, and breadcrumbs in a food processor until coarse.
Add pomegranate molasses, cumin, chili flakes, lemon juice, and salt; pulse to combine.
Drizzle in olive oil while pulsing until desired consistency.
Serve drizzled with more olive oil and a sprinkle of walnuts.
tips: The texture should be coarse, not smooth like hummus.
Pomegranate molasses gives the distinctive sweet-tart flavor.

name: Chicken Kabob
description: Juicy marinated chicken pieces threaded onto skewers and grilled over charcoal.
instructions: Bloom saffron in 1 tbsp warm water for 10 minutes.
Mix yogurt, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, grated onion, saffron water, and all spices.
Cut chicken into 2-inch cubes, coat in marinade, refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
Thread onto skewers and grill over high heat for 4-5 minutes per side.
Serve with saffron rice, grilled tomatoes, and warm flatbread.
tips: Grated onion in the marinade tenderizes the chicken and adds moisture.
Do not overcook — chicken thighs should be juicy, not dry.

name: Koshari
description: Egyptian national dish of rice, lentils, and pasta topped with spiced tomato sauce and crispy onions.
instructions: Cook lentils in water for 15 minutes until almost tender, then add rice and cook together until done.
Cook pasta separately until al dente; drain.
Thinly slice onions and fry in oil until deeply caramelized and crispy; set aside.
For tomato sauce: sauté garlic, add canned tomatoes, vinegar, cumin, chili, and salt; simmer 10 minutes.
Layer rice-lentils, then pasta, top with tomato sauce and crispy fried onions.
tips: The crispy fried onions are essential — they provide crunch and sweetness.
Each component is simple, but layered together they create a satisfying dish.

name: Stuffed Grape Leaves (Dolma)
description: Tender grape leaves filled with herbed rice, pine nuts, and lemon.
instructions: Rinse grape leaves and pat dry; if very salty, blanch briefly.
Mix raw rice with finely chopped onion, herbs, pine nuts, 1 tbsp olive oil, tomato paste, allspice, and salt.
Place a spoonful of filling on each grape leaf, fold sides in, and roll tightly.
Arrange rolls tightly in a pot, seam-side down, drizzle with remaining olive oil and lemon juice.
Add water, weigh down with a plate, cover, and simmer on low heat for 45-50 minutes until rice is cooked.
tips: Pack the rolls tightly in the pot so they hold their shape during cooking.
Serve at room temperature for the best flavor.

name: Manakish
description: Lebanese flatbread topped with za'atar and olive oil, baked until golden.
instructions: Divide dough into 4 pieces, roll each into a thin round.
Mix za'atar with olive oil and a pinch of salt into a paste.
Spread the za'atar mixture evenly over each dough round.
Bake at 230C for 8-10 minutes until dough is golden and crispy on edges.
Serve warm, folded with labneh, tomatoes, and fresh mint.
tips: The dough should be thin for a crispy result.
Za'atar and olive oil should be generously applied.

name: Hard Boiled Eggs
description: Perfectly cooked hard-boiled eggs with a fully set but not chalky yolk.
instructions: Place eggs in a single layer in a pot, cover with cold water by 1 inch.
Add salt, bring to a rolling boil over high heat.
Once boiling, remove from heat, cover, and let sit for exactly 10 minutes.
Transfer eggs to an ice bath for 5 minutes to stop cooking.
Peel under running water and serve whole, halved, or sliced.
tips: Starting in cold water and using residual heat prevents rubbery whites.
Older eggs peel more easily than fresh ones.

name: Tomato Soup
description: Silky smooth roasted tomato soup finished with a swirl of cream and fresh basil.
instructions: Heat olive oil, sauté diced onion until soft, add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Add canned tomatoes, broth, sugar, salt, and pepper; simmer for 20 minutes.
Blend until smooth using an immersion blender or regular blender.
Stir in cream, adjust seasoning.
Serve with torn basil and crusty bread for dunking.
tips: A touch of sugar balances the acidity of the tomatoes.
For a vegan version, skip the cream or use coconut cream.

name: Grilled Cheese Sandwich
description: Golden, buttery toasted bread with melted gooey cheese inside.
instructions: Butter one side of each bread slice.
Place one slice butter-side down in a pan, add sliced or grated cheese.
Top with second slice, butter-side up.
Cook on medium-low heat for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and cheese is melted.
tips: Medium-low heat is key — too hot and the bread burns before cheese melts.
A mix of cheeses (cheddar + mozzarella) gives the best flavor and stretch.

name: Pasta with Pesto
description: Al dente pasta tossed with fresh homemade basil pesto and parmesan.
instructions: Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente; reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Blend basil, pine nuts, garlic, and parmesan in a food processor; stream in olive oil.
Toss drained pasta with pesto and a splash of pasta water until coated.
Serve with extra parmesan and a drizzle of olive oil.
tips: Do not cook the pesto — it should stay raw and vibrant green.
Pasta water helps emulsify the sauce and makes it cling to the pasta.

name: Oven Roasted Chicken
description: Whole chicken roasted with herbs and garlic until skin is crispy and meat is juicy.
instructions: Pat chicken completely dry with paper towels — this is the most important step for crispy skin.
Rub softened butter mixed with minced garlic, salt, and pepper under and over the skin.
Stuff the cavity with lemon halves, thyme, and rosemary.
Truss the legs, drizzle with olive oil, and place in a roasting pan.
Roast at 200C for 1 hour 15 minutes until skin is golden and internal temperature reaches 74C; rest 15 minutes before carving.
tips: A dry surface is the number one secret to crispy skin.
Resting for 15 minutes lets juices redistribute — do not skip this.

name: Vegetable Stir-Fry
description: Quick mixed vegetable stir-fry in a savory soy-garlic sauce, served over rice.
instructions: Cut all vegetables into bite-sized pieces of similar thickness.
Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, and cornstarch with 2 tbsp water for the sauce.
Heat oil in a wok or large pan over high heat.
Add garlic and ginger, then vegetables starting with hardest (broccoli, carrot) first.
Add sauce, toss for 1-2 minutes until vegetables are crisp-tender and glazed; serve over rice.
tips: Prep everything before you start cooking — stir-frying moves fast.
Do not overcook — vegetables should be bright and still have a slight crunch.

name: Chicken Noodle Soup
description: Comforting clear broth soup with tender chicken, egg noodles, and vegetables.
instructions: Heat olive oil, sauté diced onion, carrot, and celery until softened.
Add garlic, cook 30 seconds, then pour in chicken broth with bay leaf.
Add whole chicken breast, bring to a simmer, cook for 15 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Remove chicken, shred with two forks, return to pot.
Add egg noodles, cook for 6-8 minutes until tender; season and garnish with parsley.
tips: Cooking chicken in the broth adds flavor to both.
Add noodles near the end — they absorb broth and can become mushy if overcooked.

name: Lentil Soup
description: Hearty, warming red lentil soup with cumin, garlic, and a squeeze of lemon.
instructions: Heat olive oil, sauté diced onion and carrot until soft.
Add garlic, cumin, and paprika; cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add washed lentils and broth; bring to a boil, then simmer for 20 minutes until lentils are soft.
Blend partially for a creamy-chunky texture, or fully for smooth soup.
Season with salt and lemon juice; serve with a drizzle of olive oil and crusty bread.
tips: Red lentils cook quickly and do not need soaking.
Lemon juice added at the end brightens the entire soup.

name: Spaghetti Bolognese
description: Rich Italian-style meat sauce with ground beef, tomatoes, and herbs over spaghetti.
instructions: Finely dice onion, carrot, and celery (soffritto).
Heat olive oil, sauté soffritto until soft, add garlic.
Add ground beef, break up and brown thoroughly.
Add wine, cook until reduced, then add tomatoes, tomato paste, and Italian seasoning.
Simmer on low for 30 minutes; serve over al dente spaghetti with parmesan.
tips: The soffritto (onion, carrot, celery) is the flavor base — cook it low and slow.
Simmer the sauce longer for deeper flavor — 30 minutes is the minimum.

name: Idli
description: Soft steamed rice and lentil cakes, a classic South Indian breakfast served with chutney and sambar.
instructions: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 6 hours.
Grind to a smooth batter, mix, add salt.
Ferment overnight.
Pour into idli moulds, steam for 10-12 minutes.
tips: Fermentation is key — keep in a warm spot.

name: Rava Idli
description: Soft semolina idli made without fermentation, quick and fluffy.
instructions: Roast rava lightly, cool. Mix with curd, water to a thick batter.
Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves, cashews in ghee, add to batter.
Add ENO, mix gently, pour into moulds, steam 10 min.
tips: Add ENO just before steaming for fluffy texture.

name: Oats Idli
description: Healthy idli made with oats instead of rice batter, steamed and light.
instructions: Dry roast oats and rava. Mix with curd and water.
Add grated carrots and salt.
Add ENO, pour into moulds, steam 10 min.
tips: Use instant oats for softer texture.

name: Ragi Idli
description: Nutritious finger millet idli, high in calcium and iron.
instructions: Soak urad dal 4 hours, grind smooth.
Mix with ragi flour and salt.
Ferment 8 hours. Pour into moulds, steam 12 min.
tips: Ragi idli is denser than regular — slightly thinner batter helps.

name: Masala Dosa
description: Crispy rice and lentil crepe filled with spiced potato masala.
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Temper mustard seeds, add onions, turmeric, mashed potatoes.
Spread dosa batter thin on hot tawa, drizzle oil.
Place potato filling, fold and serve with chutney and sambar.
tips: Use a well-seasoned cast iron tawa for crispiest dosa.

name: Plain Dosa
description: Thin crispy rice and lentil crepe, a staple South Indian breakfast.
instructions: Heat tawa, spread batter in thin circle.
Drizzle oil, cook until golden and crispy.
Fold and serve with chutney and sambar.
tips: Batter should be slightly thin for crispy dosa.

name: Rava Dosa
description: Crispy semolina crepe with cumin and pepper, no fermentation needed.
instructions: Mix all dry ingredients, add water to thin batter.
Pour on hot tawa in lacy pattern.
Cook until crisp, fold and serve.
tips: Batter should be very thin and pourable.

name: Moong Dal Dosa
description: Protein-rich crepe made from ground moong dal, no fermentation.
instructions: Soak moong dal and rice 4 hours. Grind to smooth batter.
Add ginger, cumin, chili. Spread on tawa, cook both sides.
tips: Soak longer for easier grinding.

name: Brown Rice Dosa
description: Healthy dosa made with brown rice for added fiber.
instructions: Soak brown rice and urad dal 6 hours.
Grind, ferment overnight.
Spread on tawa and cook until golden.
tips: Brown rice takes longer to grind — soak extra hour.

name: Wheat Dosa
description: Instant dosa made with whole wheat flour, no fermentation.
instructions: Mix flour with water, add chopped onion, cumin, chili.
Spread on tawa like dosa, cook both sides.
tips: Keep batter thin for crispy result.

name: Oats Dosa
description: Healthy instant dosa made with oats, onions, and spices.
instructions: Blend oats coarsely. Mix with rice flour, water, spices.
Spread on tawa, cook until crispy.
tips: Don't make batter too thick.

name: Mixed Dal Dosa
description: Protein-packed dosa from mixed lentils, no rice needed.
instructions: Soak all dals 4 hours. Grind to smooth batter.
Spread on tawa and cook until golden on both sides.
tips: Mix in a little rice for crispier texture.

name: Pesarattu
description: Andhra-style green gram dosa, high in protein.
instructions: Soak moong 4 hours. Grind with ginger, chilies to thick batter.
Add cumin. Spread on tawa, top with onion, cook until crisp.
tips: Serve with ginger chutney for authentic flavor.

name: Neer Dosa
description: Delicate lacy rice crepes from Karnataka, paper-thin.
instructions: Soak rice 4 hours. Grind with coconut to thin batter.
Pour on hot tawa, swirl for lacy pattern. Cover and steam 1 min.
tips: Do not flip — cook only one side.

name: Set Dosa
description: Soft spongy dosa that's thick and fluffy, served in sets of 2-3.
instructions: Add baking soda to well-fermented batter.
Pour thick rounds on tawa, don't spread too thin.
Cover and cook on low heat until set on top. Do not flip.
tips: Use extra-fermented batter for best results.

name: Millet Dosa
description: Nutritious dosa made with pearl millet (bajra), gluten-free.
instructions: Mix flours with water, add cumin, chopped onion.
Spread on hot tawa and cook both sides until golden.
tips: Millet dosa is best served hot.

name: Ragi Dosa
description: Healthy finger millet crepe rich in calcium and iron.
instructions: Mix flours with water, spices, diced onion.
Spread on tawa and cook until crispy.
tips: Add grated carrots for extra nutrition.

name: Quinoa Dosa
description: Modern healthy dosa made with quinoa and urad dal.
instructions: Soak quinoa and urad dal 4 hours.
Grind to smooth batter, ferment 6 hours.
Spread on tawa and cook until golden.
tips: Quinoa gives a nuttier flavor than rice dosa.

name: Adai Dosa
description: Thick lentil and rice crepe with mixed dals and spices.
instructions: Soak rice and all dals 2 hours.
Grind coarsely with chilies and curry leaves.
Spread thick on tawa, cook both sides with oil.
tips: Coarse grinding is key — don't make it smooth like regular dosa.

name: Uttapam
description: Thick savory rice and lentil pancake topped with vegetables.
instructions: Pour thick batter on tawa.
Top with diced onion, tomato, chili, coriander.
Cook on medium heat until bottom is golden. Flip and cook other side.
tips: Use thick, well-fermented batter for best uttapam.

name: Rava Uttapam
description: Quick semolina pancake with vegetables, no fermentation needed.
instructions: Mix rava with curd and water to thick batter. Rest 10 min.
Pour on tawa, top with veggies.
Cook both sides until golden.
tips: Let batter rest to absorb moisture.

name: Medu Vada
description: Crispy deep-fried urad dal doughnuts, a South Indian classic.
instructions: Soak urad dal 4 hours. Grind to fluffy batter (don't add much water).
Add ginger, chilies, curry leaves.
Shape into donuts, deep fry until golden brown.
tips: Batter should be thick and fluffy — the key to crispy vada.

name: Paniyaram
description: Round savory dumplings made from dosa batter in a special pan.
instructions: Temper mustard seeds and curry leaves, add to batter with diced onion.
Heat paniyaram pan, grease wells with oil.
Pour batter, cook turning until golden all around.
tips: Use well-fermented batter for fluffy paniyaram.

name: Appam
description: Lacy bowl-shaped rice crepe with soft fluffy center, from Kerala.
instructions: Soak and grind rice. Add coconut milk, yeast, sugar. Ferment 6 hours.
Pour in appam pan, swirl for thin edges.
Cover and cook until center is fluffy and edges are lacy.
tips: The pan must be very hot for lacy edges.

name: Podi Idli
description: Leftover idli tossed with spicy podi (gun powder) and ghee.
instructions: Cut idlis into quarters.
Heat ghee and oil, toss idli pieces.
Add podi powder, toss well until coated.
tips: Use day-old idlis for best texture.

name: Punugulu
description: Quick deep-fried dosa batter fritters, crispy outside and soft inside.
instructions: Add diced onion, chili, curry leaves to thick batter.
Drop spoonfuls into hot oil.
Fry until golden brown and crispy.
tips: Batter should be thick for round shape.

name: Akki Roti
description: Karnataka-style rice flour flatbread with vegetables.
instructions: Mix rice flour with finely chopped vegetables, salt, and water.
Pat into thin rounds on banana leaf or plastic.
Cook on tawa with oil until both sides are golden.
tips: Add sesame seeds for extra flavor.

name: Ven Pongal
description: Comforting South Indian rice-lentil porridge tempered with pepper and cumin.
instructions: Dry roast moong dal. Cook rice and dal together until mushy.
Temper pepper, cumin, curry leaves, cashews in ghee.
Pour tempering over pongal, mix well.
tips: Cook until creamy consistency — add more water if needed.

name: Idli Upma
description: Leftover idli crumbled and tempered with spices — zero waste breakfast.
instructions: Crumble idlis by hand.
Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves, add onion and chili.
Add crumbled idli, toss well. Squeeze lemon juice.
tips: Great way to use day-old idlis.

name: Coconut Chutney
description: Fresh coconut chutney with tempering, essential South Indian accompaniment.
instructions: Grind coconut, chana dal, chili, ginger with water.
Temper mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil.
Pour tempering over chutney, mix.
tips: Add a small piece of raw mango for tang.

name: Rava Kesari
description: Sweet semolina halwa with ghee, cashews, and saffron.
instructions: Roast rava in ghee until golden.
Add hot water carefully, stir to avoid lumps.
Add sugar, saffron, cardamom. Cook until thick. Garnish with cashews.
tips: Use generous ghee for authentic taste.

name: Aloo Paratha
description: Stuffed potato flatbread cooked with ghee, a Punjabi breakfast staple.
instructions: Knead dough. Boil, mash potatoes with spices.
Stuff dough balls with potato mixture, roll out.
Cook on tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
tips: Roll evenly to prevent filling from bursting out.

name: Gobi Paratha
description: Spiced cauliflower stuffed whole wheat flatbread.
instructions: Grate cauliflower, squeeze out water. Mix with spices.
Stuff dough, roll out.
Cook on tawa with ghee until golden.
tips: Squeeze cauliflower well to prevent soggy parathas.

name: Mooli Paratha
description: Spiced grated radish stuffed flatbread.
instructions: Grate radish, squeeze water. Mix with spices.
Stuff dough, roll, cook with ghee.
tips: Salt the radish and drain to remove excess moisture.

name: Palak Paratha
description: Spinach-infused green flatbread, nutritious and flavorful.
instructions: Blanch spinach, puree. Knead into dough with spices.
Roll out and cook on tawa with ghee.
tips: Don't add too much spinach puree or dough gets sticky.

name: Methi Paratha
description: Fenugreek leaf flatbread, aromatic and nutritious.
instructions: Chop methi finely. Knead into dough with spices.
Roll and cook on tawa with ghee.
tips: Add a pinch of sugar to reduce bitterness of methi.

name: Pumpkin Paratha
description: Sweet and savory stuffed pumpkin flatbread.
instructions: Grate pumpkin, cook until dry. Season with spices.
Stuff dough with pumpkin mixture, roll and cook with ghee.
tips: Cook filling until completely dry.

name: Mix Veg Paratha
description: Flatbread stuffed with mixed vegetables and spices.
instructions: Finely chop and sauté vegetables with spices.
Stuff dough, roll, cook on tawa with ghee.
tips: Chop veggies very fine for easy stuffing.

name: Plain Paratha
description: Layered whole wheat flatbread cooked with ghee.
instructions: Knead soft dough. Roll, apply ghee, fold into layers.
Roll again, cook on tawa with ghee until golden layers form.
tips: Fold and roll multiple times for flaky layers.

name: Thepla
description: Gujarati spiced fenugreek flatbread, great for travel.
instructions: Mix flour with chopped methi, curd, turmeric, salt. Knead soft dough.
Roll thin, cook on tawa with oil until light brown spots appear.
tips: Stays fresh for days — ideal travel food.

name: Poori
description: Deep-fried puffed whole wheat bread, festive breakfast.
instructions: Knead firm dough with flour, salt, a little oil.
Roll into small circles.
Deep fry in hot oil until they puff up and turn golden.
tips: Oil must be very hot for pooris to puff.

name: Bhatura
description: Deep-fried leavened bread, served with chole.
instructions: Mix flour, curd, baking soda, sugar, salt. Knead soft dough, rest 2 hours.
Roll into oval shapes.
Deep fry until puffed and golden.
tips: Resting the dough is essential for soft bhatura.

name: Chole Bhature
description: Spiced chickpea curry with deep-fried bread, a Delhi classic.
instructions: Soak and pressure cook chickpeas. Make chole gravy with onion-tomato-spice base.
Knead bhatura dough, rest, roll and deep fry.
Serve hot bhatura with chole.
tips: Soak chickpeas overnight and add tea bags while boiling for dark color.

name: Poha
description: Spiced flattened rice with mustard seeds, onions, and peanuts.
instructions: Rinse poha, drain, let soften 5 min.
Temper mustard seeds, fry peanuts, add curry leaves and onion.
Add poha, turmeric, salt. Toss gently, squeeze lemon.
tips: Don't over-soak or it becomes mushy.

name: Upma
description: Savory semolina porridge tempered with spices and vegetables.
instructions: Dry roast rava until fragrant.
Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves, add onion and chili.
Add water, salt, bring to boil. Add rava, stir continuously until thick.
tips: Stir continuously to prevent lumps.

name: Bread Upma
description: Quick breakfast made with bread cubes tempered with spices.
instructions: Cut bread into cubes.
Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves. Add onion, chili.
Add bread cubes, toss with turmeric and salt.
tips: Use day-old bread for best results.

name: Semiya Upma
description: Vermicelli upma, a quick and light South Indian breakfast.
instructions: Roast vermicelli in ghee until golden.
Temper mustard seeds, peanuts, curry leaves. Add onion.
Add water, salt. Add roasted vermicelli, cover and cook 5 min.
tips: Roast vermicelli well to prevent mushy texture.

name: Oats Upma
description: Healthy upma made with oats instead of semolina.
instructions: Dry roast oats 2 min.
Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves, add vegetables.
Add water, oats, cook 3-4 min until thick.
tips: Don't over-cook or oats become gummy.

name: Tomato Upma
description: Tangy tomato-flavored semolina upma.
instructions: Roast rava. Temper spices, add onion and tomato.
Add water, salt. Add rava, stir until thick.
tips: Use ripe red tomatoes for best flavor.

name: Aval Upma
description: Upma made with flattened rice (poha) and spices.
instructions: Rinse thick poha, drain.
Temper spices, add onion and peanuts.
Add poha, turmeric, salt. Toss and cook 3 min.
tips: Use thick poha variety for this.

name: Besan Chilla
description: Savory gram flour pancake with onions and spices.
instructions: Mix besan with water, spices, onion to smooth batter.
Pour on hot tawa, spread thin.
Cook both sides with a little oil until golden.
tips: Add finely grated vegetables for extra nutrition.

name: Moong Dal Chilla
description: Protein-rich savory pancake from ground moong lentils.
instructions: Soak moong dal 4 hours. Grind to smooth batter.
Add ginger, chili, cumin.
Spread on tawa, cook both sides until golden.
tips: Soak overnight for easiest grinding.

name: Masoor Dal Chilla
description: Savory red lentil pancakes, quick and protein-packed.
instructions: Soak masoor dal 2 hours. Grind to batter.
Mix in diced onion, cumin, coriander.
Spread on tawa and cook both sides.
tips: Masoor grinds faster than other dals.

name: Oats Chilla
description: Healthy savory pancake made with oats and besan.
instructions: Blend oats. Mix with besan, water, veggies, spices.
Spread on tawa, cook both sides.
tips: Great for kids — hide vegetables in the batter.

name: Egg Bhurji
description: Indian scrambled eggs with onions, tomatoes, and spices.
instructions: Beat eggs. Sauté onion, tomato, chili with turmeric.
Add beaten eggs, scramble until cooked.
Garnish with coriander.
tips: Don't overcook — keep eggs slightly soft.

name: Sabudana Khichdi
description: Fluffy sago pearls with peanuts and potato, a fasting breakfast.
instructions: Soak sabudana 4-6 hours. Drain well.
Roast and crush peanuts. Cube and fry potato.
Temper cumin, add sabudana, peanuts, potato. Toss gently.
tips: Sabudana must be soaked properly — each pearl should be separate.

name: Dalia
description: Broken wheat porridge cooked with vegetables and mild spices.
instructions: Dry roast dalia until nutty.
Temper cumin, sauté onion, tomato, peas.
Add water, dalia, cook until soft and fluffy.
tips: Roasting dalia enhances flavor and keeps grains separate.

name: Savory Masala French Toast
description: Indian-spiced French toast with chili and coriander.
instructions: Beat eggs with finely chopped chili, onion, coriander, salt.
Dip bread slices in egg mixture.
Pan fry on both sides until golden.
tips: Use thick bread for better absorption.

name: Bread Pakora
description: Bread slices dipped in spiced gram flour batter and deep fried.
instructions: Make potato stuffing with spices. Sandwich between bread slices.
Make besan batter. Dip sandwiches in batter.
Deep fry until golden and crispy.
tips: Squeeze bread slightly before dipping so it doesn't absorb too much oil.

name: Bombay Sandwich
description: Layered vegetable sandwich with chutneys, a Mumbai street classic.
instructions: Spread chutney on bread. Layer sliced veggies and potato.
Press together, toast on tawa with butter.
Cut diagonally and serve.
tips: Use thick-sliced bread for sturdier sandwich.

name: Kolkata Egg Roll
description: Egg-wrapped paratha roll with onions and chutney, street food style.
instructions: Cook paratha on tawa. Pour beaten egg on top, flip to set.
Add sliced onion, chutney, lemon juice.
Roll up tightly and serve.
tips: Use a hot tawa so egg sets quickly.

name: Handvo
description: Gujarati savory rice and lentil cake with vegetables.
instructions: Soak rice and dals, grind to batter, ferment overnight.
Add grated bottle gourd, spices, baking soda.
Pour in greased pan, top with sesame seeds. Bake or cook on stovetop.
tips: Can be baked in oven at 375°F for 30 min.

name: Dhokla
description: Steamed fermented gram flour cake, light and spongy Gujarati snack.
instructions: Mix besan, curd, turmeric, water to smooth batter.
Add ENO, mix gently, pour into greased plate, steam 12 min.
Temper mustard seeds and curry leaves, pour over dhokla.
tips: Add ENO just before steaming.

name: Masala Chai
description: Indian spiced milk tea with ginger and cardamom.
instructions: Boil water with crushed ginger and cardamom.
Add tea leaves, simmer 2 min.
Add milk and sugar, bring to boil twice, strain and serve.
tips: Crush cardamom and ginger for maximum flavor.

name: Overnight Oats
description: No-cook oats soaked in milk overnight with fruits and nuts.
instructions: Mix oats, milk, yogurt, chia seeds, honey in a jar.
Top with berries.
Refrigerate overnight, eat cold in the morning.
tips: Prepare 2-3 jars at once for easy weekday breakfasts.

name: Baked Oatmeal
description: Warm oven-baked oatmeal with banana and cinnamon, feeds a crowd.
instructions: Mash bananas. Mix all ingredients.
Pour into greased baking dish.
Bake at 375°F for 30 min until set and golden.
tips: Make ahead — reheat portions through the week.

name: Classic Oatmeal
description: Simple stovetop oatmeal with honey and banana.
instructions: Bring liquid to boil, add oats.
Cook 5 min stirring occasionally.
Top with sliced banana, honey, cinnamon.
tips: Use steel-cut oats for chewier texture (cook longer).

name: Savory Oatmeal
description: Oatmeal topped with a fried egg, cheese, and herbs — savory twist.
instructions: Cook oats in water with salt.
Fry an egg separately.
Top oatmeal with egg, cheese, pepper, herbs.
tips: Add sautéed spinach for extra nutrition.

name: Homemade Granola
description: Crunchy oven-baked oat clusters with nuts and honey.
instructions: Mix oats, nuts, melted coconut oil, honey, vanilla, cinnamon.
Spread on baking sheet, press flat.
Bake at 325°F for 25 min, stir once. Cool completely for clusters.
tips: Don't stir after baking for big crunchy clusters.

name: Cinnamon Quinoa Breakfast Bowl
description: Warm quinoa cooked like oatmeal with cinnamon and fruit.
instructions: Cook quinoa in milk with cinnamon.
Sweeten with maple syrup.
Top with berries and sliced almonds.
tips: Rinse quinoa well to remove bitterness.

name: Chia Seed Pudding
description: Creamy chia pudding soaked in milk overnight with vanilla.
instructions: Mix chia seeds, milk, vanilla, honey.
Stir well, refrigerate 4 hours or overnight.
Top with fresh fruit before serving.
tips: Stir after 10 min to prevent clumping.

name: Mango Chia Pudding
description: Tropical chia pudding layered with fresh mango puree.
instructions: Mix chia seeds with coconut milk and honey.
Refrigerate 4 hours.
Blend mango, layer with chia pudding.
tips: Use ripe Alphonso mango for best sweetness.

name: Classic Omelette
description: Fluffy folded omelette with cheese and herbs.
instructions: Beat eggs with salt and pepper.
Melt butter in pan, pour eggs.
When nearly set, add cheese and herbs, fold in half.
tips: Use medium-low heat for fluffy omelette.

name: Scrambled Eggs
description: Soft creamy scrambled eggs, a breakfast essential.
instructions: Beat eggs with milk, salt, pepper.
Melt butter on low heat, add eggs.
Stir gently with spatula, remove when just set.
tips: Low heat and gentle stirring = creamiest scramble.

name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in spiced tomato-pepper sauce, a Middle Eastern classic.
instructions: Sauté onion and pepper in olive oil. Add spices.
Add crushed tomatoes, simmer 10 min.
Make wells, crack eggs in, cover and cook until set.
tips: Serve with crusty bread for dipping.

name: Frittata
description: Italian open-faced omelette baked with vegetables and cheese.
instructions: Sauté vegetables in oven-safe pan.
Pour beaten eggs over, top with cheese.
Cook on stove 3 min, then broil 5 min until puffed and golden.
tips: Great for using up leftover vegetables.

name: Egg Muffins
description: Mini egg cups baked with vegetables, perfect for meal prep.
instructions: Beat eggs, mix in chopped vegetables and cheese.
Pour into greased muffin tin.
Bake at 375°F for 18-20 min.
tips: Freeze for up to 1 month — reheat in microwave.

name: Crustless Quiche
description: Light egg and vegetable quiche without the pastry crust.
instructions: Sauté vegetables. Beat eggs with milk.
Layer veggies and cheese in baking dish. Pour egg mixture.
Bake at 375°F for 25-30 min until set.
tips: Let rest 5 min before cutting.

name: Egg Toast
description: Simple fried or scrambled egg on buttered toast.
instructions: Toast bread. Fry or scramble eggs with butter.
Place eggs on toast, season.
tips: Runny yolk on toast is chef's kiss.

name: Strawberry Banana Smoothie
description: Classic fruit smoothie, naturally sweet and creamy.
instructions: Add all ingredients to blender.
Blend until smooth.
Pour and serve immediately.
tips: Use frozen fruit for thicker smoothie.

name: Avocado Smoothie
description: Creamy avocado smoothie with banana and spinach.
instructions: Blend all ingredients until creamy.
Adjust thickness with more milk if needed.
tips: Avocado makes it incredibly creamy.

name: Peanut Butter Banana Smoothie
description: Protein-rich smoothie with peanut butter and banana.
instructions: Blend all ingredients until smooth.
tips: Add a scoop of protein powder for post-workout breakfast.

name: Green Smoothie
description: Nutrient-packed smoothie with spinach, banana, and fruits.
instructions: Blend spinach and water first until smooth.
Add remaining fruits and ginger, blend again.
tips: Spinach flavor is undetectable once blended.

name: Mango Smoothie
description: Tropical mango smoothie with yogurt, perfect summer breakfast.
instructions: Blend mango, yogurt, milk, honey until smooth.
tips: Use frozen mango for thicker texture.

name: Blueberry Smoothie
description: Antioxidant-rich blueberry smoothie with banana.
instructions: Blend all ingredients until smooth.
tips: Frozen blueberries work just as well.

name: Coffee Smoothie
description: Energizing breakfast smoothie with coffee and banana.
instructions: Blend all ingredients until smooth and frothy.
tips: Freeze coffee in ice cube trays for thicker smoothie.

name: Classic Pancakes
description: Fluffy American-style pancakes with maple syrup.
instructions: Mix dry ingredients. Whisk wet ingredients separately.
Combine, don't overmix (lumps are fine).
Cook on griddle until bubbles form, flip, cook until golden.
tips: Don't press down on pancakes while cooking.

name: Banana Pancakes
description: Naturally sweet pancakes made with mashed banana.
instructions: Mash bananas. Mix with eggs, oats, cinnamon, baking powder.
Cook small pancakes on medium heat.
Flip when bubbles form.
tips: Riper bananas = sweeter pancakes.

name: Blueberry Pancakes
description: Fluffy pancakes studded with fresh blueberries.
instructions: Mix dry ingredients. Add wet, fold in blueberries.
Cook on griddle, flip when bubbles form.
tips: Don't smash blueberries — fold gently.

name: Oatmeal Pancakes
description: Hearty whole grain pancakes made with oats.
instructions: Blend oats into flour. Mix with remaining ingredients.
Cook on medium heat, flip when set.
tips: Blend oats fine for smooth pancakes, coarse for rustic.

name: French Toast
description: Bread slices soaked in egg-milk mixture, pan-fried golden.
instructions: Beat eggs with milk, cinnamon, vanilla.
Dip bread slices to coat evenly.
Cook in butter until golden on both sides.
tips: Use thick bread like brioche for best results.

name: Homemade Waffles
description: Crispy on the outside, fluffy inside — classic waffles.
instructions: Mix dry ingredients. Whisk wet ingredients.
Combine gently. Pour into preheated waffle iron.
Cook until steam stops and waffle is golden.
tips: Separate eggs, whip whites for extra fluffy waffles.

name: Avocado Toast
description: Smashed avocado on toast with lemon, chili flakes, and salt.
instructions: Toast bread. Mash avocado with lemon, salt, chili.
Spread on toast.
tips: Top with a poached egg for extra protein.

name: Breakfast Sandwich
description: Egg and cheese sandwich on toasted bread.
instructions: Toast bread. Fry egg.
Layer egg, cheese, spinach on toast.
tips: Add avocado or tomato for extra flavor.

name: Healthy Banana Bread
description: Moist banana bread made with whole wheat and less sugar.
instructions: Mash bananas. Mix wet ingredients.
Fold in dry ingredients.
Bake at 350°F for 45 min.
tips: Use very ripe (spotty) bananas for best sweetness.

name: Zucchini Bread
description: Moist quick bread with grated zucchini and warm spices.
instructions: Grate zucchini, squeeze water.
Mix wet and dry ingredients separately, combine.
Bake at 350°F for 50 min.
tips: Don't skip squeezing the zucchini moisture.

name: Blueberry Muffins
description: Classic fluffy muffins bursting with blueberries.
instructions: Mix dry ingredients. Combine wet ingredients.
Fold together, add blueberries.
Fill muffin cups 2/3 full, bake 375°F for 22 min.
tips: Toss blueberries in flour to prevent sinking.

name: Healthy Banana Muffins
description: Naturally sweetened banana muffins with oats.
instructions: Blend oats into flour. Mash bananas.
Mix all ingredients. Spoon into muffin tin.
Bake 350°F for 18-20 min.
tips: These are lower sugar — sweetened by banana and a little honey.

name: Apple Muffins
description: Warm spiced apple muffins with cinnamon.
instructions: Dice apple. Mix dry ingredients.
Combine wet, fold in apples.
Bake 375°F for 22 min.
tips: Granny Smith apples hold up best in baking.

name: Granola Bars
description: Chewy homemade granola bars with oats, honey, and nuts.
instructions: Heat honey and peanut butter until smooth.
Mix with oats, nuts, cranberries.
Press into pan, bake 325°F for 20 min. Cool and cut.
tips: Press firmly for bars that hold together.

name: Energy Balls
description: No-bake protein bites with oats, peanut butter, and chocolate.
instructions: Mix all ingredients.
Roll into balls.
Refrigerate 30 min before serving.
tips: Make a big batch — keeps in fridge for a week.

name: Blueberry Scones
description: Buttery tender scones with blueberries.
instructions: Cut butter into flour. Add sugar, baking powder.
Fold in blueberries, add cream until dough forms.
Shape, cut into wedges, bake 400°F for 15 min.
tips: Cold butter is essential for flaky scones.

name: Sweet Potato Hash
description: Crispy cubed sweet potatoes with peppers and onions.
instructions: Cube sweet potatoes small.
Cook in oil on medium-high until crispy, 12-15 min.
Add peppers and onion, cook 5 more min. Season.
tips: Don't overcrowd the pan for crispiest hash.

name: Breakfast Tacos
description: Soft tortillas filled with scrambled eggs and salsa.
instructions: Scramble eggs. Warm tortillas.
Fill with eggs, beans, cheese, avocado, salsa.
tips: Corn tortillas for gluten-free option.

name: Breakfast Burrito
description: Rolled flour tortilla filled with eggs, beans, and cheese.
instructions: Scramble eggs. Warm tortilla.
Layer beans, eggs, cheese, salsa, sour cream.
Roll up tightly.
tips: Wrap in foil for on-the-go breakfast.

name: Farmers Market Breakfast Bowl
description: Grain bowl with roasted vegetables, greens, and egg.
instructions: Cook grain. Roast cubed sweet potato 20 min.
Sauté kale. Fry egg.
Assemble bowl with all components.
tips: Prep grains and roast veggies ahead for quick assembly.

name: Yogurt Bowl
description: Creamy yogurt topped with granola, fruits, and honey.
instructions: Spoon yogurt into bowl.
Top with granola, sliced fruit, drizzle honey.
tips: Layer for best texture — eat immediately so granola stays crunchy.

name: Fruit Salad
description: Fresh seasonal fruit salad with a squeeze of lime and mint.
instructions: Chop all fruits into bite-sized pieces.
Toss with lime juice.
Garnish with mint leaves.
tips: Use seasonal fruits for best flavor and price.

name: Kale Potato Paratha
description: Nutritious stuffed paratha with kale and potato filling.
instructions: Blanch and chop kale. Mash boiled potatoes, mix with kale and spices.
Stuff dough, roll, cook on tawa with ghee.
tips: Blanching kale removes bitterness.

name: Thalipeeth
description: Maharashtrian multi-grain savory flatbread.
instructions: Mix flour with chopped onion, coriander, cumin, water.
Pat directly on tawa into thick round.
Make a hole in center, add oil. Cook both sides.
tips: Use multi-grain flour mix for authentic taste.

name: Kothu Parotta
description: Shredded flaky parotta tossed with eggs and spices, South Indian street food.
instructions: Shred parotta into small pieces.
Scramble eggs separately. Sauté onion, chili, curry leaves.
Add shredded parotta, eggs, soy sauce. Toss on high heat.
tips: Use leftover parotta for best results.

name: Sheera
description: Sweet semolina dessert with ghee, nuts, and saffron — same as Rava Kesari.
instructions: Roast rava in ghee. Add hot milk carefully.
Cook until thick, add sugar, cardamom.
Garnish with fried cashews.
tips: Ghee and sugar make this rich — best as occasional treat.

name: Appe
description: Round savory dumplings cooked in a special pan, like mini uttapam.
instructions: Mix finely diced veggies into dosa batter.
Heat appe pan, add oil in each cavity.
Pour batter, cook on low heat, turn for even cooking.
tips: Use a well-seasoned appe pan for non-stick results.

name: Ragi Roti
description: Nutritious finger millet flatbread, high in calcium.
instructions: Mix flour with diced onion, cumin, coriander, water.
Pat into rounds on tawa.
Cook both sides until done.
tips: Press directly on tawa — ragi dough doesn't roll well.

name: Dudhi Muthiya
description: Steamed bottle gourd dumplings from Gujarat.
instructions: Grate bottle gourd, mix with flours, spices.
Shape into logs, steam 15 min.
Slice and temper with mustard seeds and sesame.
tips: Squeeze excess water from gourd.

name: Kothimbir Vadi
description: Crispy coriander fritters from Maharashtra.
instructions: Mix chopped coriander with flours, spices, water to thick batter.
Steam 15 min, cool, cut into squares.
Shallow fry until crispy.
tips: Steam first then fry for best texture.

name: Cheese Dosa
description: Crispy dosa topped with grated cheese, a kids' favorite.
instructions: Spread dosa batter thin on hot tawa.
Sprinkle grated cheese on top.
Cook until crispy and cheese melts. Fold and serve.
tips: Use a blend of mozzarella and cheddar.

name: Oats Egg Omelette
description: Quick protein-rich omelette with oats mixed in.
instructions: Beat eggs with oats, chopped onion, chili.
Pour on heated pan, cook until set.
Flip and cook other side.
tips: Oats add fiber without changing taste much.

name: Spinach Omelette
description: Eggs cooked with fresh spinach and onions.
instructions: Beat eggs. Sauté spinach and onion briefly.
Pour eggs over, cook until set, fold.
tips: Wilt spinach first so it doesn't release water into the eggs.

name: Kung Pao Chicken
description: Sichuan stir-fried chicken with peanuts, dried chilies, and Sichuan peppercorns in a savory-sweet sauce.
instructions: Cut chicken into 2cm cubes, marinate with 1 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tsp cornstarch for 15 minutes.
Mix remaining soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, remaining cornstarch, and 2 tbsp water into a sauce.
Heat oil in a wok until smoking, stir-fry chicken until golden; set aside.
Toast Sichuan peppercorns and dried chilies in the wok until fragrant.
Add garlic and ginger, stir-fry 30 seconds, return chicken, pour in sauce.
Toss until sauce thickens, add peanuts and spring onions, drizzle sesame oil.
tips: Toast Sichuan peppercorns on low heat to avoid burning.
Chinkiang vinegar is essential for authentic flavor.

name: Mapo Tofu
description: Soft tofu in a fiery Sichuan sauce with ground pork, doubanjiang, and Sichuan peppercorns.
instructions: Cut tofu into 2cm cubes, blanch in salted water for 2 minutes; drain gently.
Heat oil in wok, brown ground pork until crispy.
Add doubanjiang and fermented black beans, stir-fry 1 minute until oil turns red.
Add garlic and ginger, stir 30 seconds, then pour in chicken stock and soy sauce.
Slide in tofu gently, simmer 5 minutes. Mix cornstarch with water, stir in to thicken.
Sprinkle Sichuan peppercorn powder and spring onions, serve over steamed rice.
tips: Blanching tofu removes excess water and firms it up.
Add the doubanjiang to oil first to release its red color and flavor.

name: General Tso's Chicken
description: Crispy battered chicken in a sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy sauce with steamed broccoli.
instructions: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces, coat with egg and cornstarch.
Deep fry in batches at 180C until golden and crispy, about 4 minutes; drain.
Mix soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, hoisin, and 3 tbsp water for the sauce.
In a clean wok, stir-fry garlic, ginger, and dried chilies for 30 seconds.
Pour in sauce, bring to a simmer, add fried chicken and toss to coat.
Drizzle sesame oil, serve with steamed broccoli and rice.
tips: Double-frying makes the coating extra crispy — fry once, rest 5 min, fry again.
The sauce should coat the chicken in a thin, glossy layer, not pool at the bottom.

name: Sweet and Sour Pork
description: Crispy battered pork pieces with bell peppers and pineapple in a tangy sweet and sour sauce.
instructions: Cut pork into 3cm cubes, coat with beaten egg then cornstarch.
Deep fry at 180C until golden and crispy, about 4-5 minutes; drain.
Mix ketchup, rice vinegar, sugar, soy sauce, and 3 tbsp water for the sauce.
Stir-fry bell pepper, onion, and pineapple for 2 minutes in 1 tbsp oil.
Pour in sauce, bring to a boil, add fried pork and toss until coated.
Serve immediately over steamed rice.
tips: Fry pork in small batches to keep oil temperature consistent.
Add pineapple last to keep its texture firm.

name: Egg Fried Rice
description: Classic Chinese fried rice with egg, spring onions, and a touch of soy sauce.
instructions: Beat eggs with a pinch of salt. Heat oil in a wok until smoking.
Scramble eggs quickly, breaking into small pieces; set aside.
Add more oil, stir-fry garlic for 15 seconds, add rice and toss vigorously.
Add soy sauce, white pepper, and peas; stir-fry 2-3 minutes until rice is slightly crispy.
Return eggs, add spring onions and sesame oil, toss and serve.
tips: Day-old refrigerated rice is essential — fresh rice becomes mushy.
Keep the wok screaming hot for the best fried rice texture.

name: Hot and Sour Soup
description: Spicy and tangy soup with tofu, mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and a silky egg ribbon.
instructions: Bring chicken stock to a boil. Add sliced mushrooms and bamboo shoots, simmer 3 minutes.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, and white pepper.
Add diced tofu, simmer 2 minutes.
Mix cornstarch with 3 tbsp water, stir into soup to thicken.
Beat egg, slowly drizzle into simmering soup while stirring gently to form ribbons.
Finish with sesame oil and chili oil, serve hot.
tips: Add vinegar after turning off heat for a sharper tang.
Stir in one direction when adding egg for clean ribbons.

name: Wonton Soup
description: Delicate pork and shrimp wontons in a light ginger-scallion broth.
instructions: Mince shrimp, mix with pork, 1 tbsp soy sauce, ginger, sesame oil, white pepper, and chopped spring onion.
Place 1 tsp filling in center of each wrapper, wet edges, fold into triangle and seal.
Bring chicken stock to a boil, season with remaining soy sauce.
Cook wontons in boiling stock for 3-4 minutes until they float.
Add halved bok choy, cook 1 minute. Serve in bowls with broth and garnish with spring onion.
tips: Do not overfill wontons or they will burst while cooking.
A splash of sesame oil in the broth adds depth.

name: Dan Dan Noodles
description: Spicy Sichuan noodles with ground pork, sesame paste, and chili oil in a numbing sauce.
instructions: Cook noodles according to package directions; drain.
Brown ground pork in oil with minced garlic and preserved mustard greens until crispy.
Mix sesame paste, soy sauce, chili oil, vinegar, sugar, Sichuan peppercorn powder, and 3 tbsp hot water in each bowl.
Divide noodles into bowls, top with pork mixture.
Garnish with chopped spring onions and extra chili oil to taste.
tips: Thin out the sesame paste with hot water for a smooth sauce.
Ya cai (preserved mustard greens) adds authentic flavor — substitute with pickled vegetables if unavailable.

name: Char Siu Pork
description: Cantonese BBQ pork glazed with a sweet honey-hoisin marinade, roasted until caramelized.
instructions: Cut pork into thick strips about 3cm wide.
Mix hoisin, soy sauce, oyster sauce, honey, five-spice, minced garlic, fermented tofu, and wine.
Marinate pork for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Roast on a wire rack at 200C for 15 minutes, then reduce to 180C.
Baste with marinade every 10 minutes for another 20-25 minutes until caramelized and charred at edges.
Rest 5 minutes, slice and serve with steamed rice.
tips: The red color traditionally comes from fermented red tofu, not food coloring.
Broil for the last 2 minutes for extra charring.

name: Peking Duck Wraps
description: Crispy roasted duck slices with hoisin sauce, cucumber, and spring onion in thin pancakes.
instructions: Pat duck dry, rub inside with five-spice and salt. Pour boiling water over skin twice to tighten it.
Brush skin with honey and soy sauce mixture, hang or refrigerate uncovered overnight to dry.
Roast on a rack at 180C for 1.5-2 hours, basting every 30 minutes.
Carve skin and meat into thin slices.
Serve with warm Mandarin pancakes, hoisin sauce, cucumber batons, and spring onion shreds.
Wrap a few slices of duck, sauce, and vegetables in each pancake.
tips: Drying the skin overnight is the secret to crackly crisp skin.
Serve the skin separately from the meat for the best texture experience.

name: Chinese Braised Eggplant
description: Silky eggplant braised in a savory garlic sauce with soy, vinegar, and a hint of sugar.
instructions: Cut eggplant into thick batons. Heat oil in a wok, fry eggplant until golden and soft; set aside.
In the same wok, stir-fry garlic, ginger, and chili flakes for 30 seconds.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, and 3 tbsp water; bring to a simmer.
Return eggplant, braise for 3-4 minutes until sauce is absorbed.
Mix cornstarch with 1 tbsp water, stir in to thicken. Finish with sesame oil and spring onion.
tips: Chinese eggplants work best — they are thinner and creamier than globe eggplants.
Do not skip frying — it gives the eggplant a silky, melt-in-mouth texture.

name: Cashew Chicken
description: Tender chicken stir-fried with roasted cashews, vegetables, and a light soy-oyster sauce.
instructions: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces, toss with 1 tbsp soy sauce and cornstarch.
Heat oil in a wok, stir-fry chicken until golden; set aside.
Stir-fry garlic, onion, bell pepper, and celery for 2 minutes.
Return chicken, add remaining soy sauce and oyster sauce, toss 1 minute.
Add cashews, drizzle sesame oil, toss and serve over rice.
tips: Add cashews at the very end to keep them crunchy.
Toast raw cashews in a dry pan first if not using pre-roasted.

name: Black Pepper Beef
description: Tender beef strips stir-fried with onions and bell peppers in a bold black pepper sauce.
instructions: Slice beef thinly against the grain, marinate with 1 tbsp soy sauce and cornstarch for 15 minutes.
Heat oil in a wok until smoking, sear beef in batches until browned; set aside.
Add butter, stir-fry sliced onion and bell pepper for 2 minutes.
Add garlic and freshly cracked black pepper, stir 30 seconds.
Return beef, add remaining soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sugar; toss for 1 minute.
Serve immediately over steamed rice.
tips: Slice beef against the grain for tenderness.
Coarsely crack black pepper fresh — pre-ground lacks the punch.

name: Ma La Xiang Guo
description: Sichuan dry pot with assorted vegetables and protein in a numbing-spicy chili sauce.
instructions: Slice all vegetables and protein into bite-sized pieces. Blanch hard vegetables (lotus root, potato, broccoli) briefly.
Heat oil in a wok, toast Sichuan peppercorns and dried chilies until fragrant.
Add doubanjiang, stir-fry 1 minute until oil turns red.
Add garlic and cumin seeds, stir 30 seconds.
Add protein first, cook 3 minutes, then add all vegetables and stir-fry 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce, toss vigorously until everything is coated. Serve immediately.
tips: Blanch harder vegetables first so everything finishes cooking at the same time.
Adjust chili and peppercorn quantities to your spice tolerance.

name: Twice Cooked Pork
description: Sichuan classic of boiled then stir-fried pork belly with leeks, doubanjiang, and fermented black beans.
instructions: Boil pork belly with ginger slices for 20 minutes until just cooked. Cool and slice thinly.
Heat wok with oil, add pork slices and stir-fry until edges curl and fat renders.
Push pork aside, add doubanjiang and fermented black beans; stir-fry 1 minute.
Add shaoxing wine and soy sauce, toss pork to coat.
Add sliced leek and bell pepper, stir-fry 2 minutes until just wilted.
Add sugar, toss, and serve with steamed rice.
tips: Slice the pork as thin as possible for the best texture.
The pork slices should curl at the edges — that is the sign of proper cooking.

name: Steamed Fish with Ginger and Scallion
description: Delicate whole steamed fish draped with ginger, scallion, and sizzling hot soy-sesame oil.

ingredients.name: whole white fish (sea bass or snapper)

instructions: Score fish on both sides, rub with wine and salt. Place on a plate with ginger slices underneath.
Steam over high heat for 8-10 minutes until fish flakes easily.
Discard any liquid on the plate and the steaming ginger.
Top fish with fresh julienned ginger and spring onion shreds.
Heat vegetable oil and sesame oil until smoking, pour sizzling oil over the fish.
Drizzle soy sauce mixed with sugar, garnish with cilantro, and serve immediately.
tips: Do not overcook — the fish should just flake. Start checking at 8 minutes.
The sizzling oil is essential — it cooks the aromatics and releases their fragrance.

name: Mongolian Beef
description: Crispy beef strips in a sweet and savory soy-brown sugar sauce with scallions.
instructions: Slice flank steak thinly against the grain, toss with cornstarch until fully coated.
Deep fry beef in batches at 180C until crispy, about 2 minutes; drain.
In a clean wok, sauté garlic and ginger in 1 tbsp oil for 30 seconds.
Add soy sauce, brown sugar, hoisin, and water; bring to a simmer.
Add fried beef, toss to coat in sauce. Add spring onion cut into 5cm lengths.
Drizzle sesame oil and serve over steamed rice.
tips: Slice against the grain for tender beef.
The beef should be coated in a sticky, glossy sauce — not swimming in liquid.

name: Shrimp Lo Mein
description: Egg noodles tossed with plump shrimp, vegetables, and a savory soy-sesame sauce.
instructions: Cook noodles until al dente, drain and toss with sesame oil.
Heat oil in a wok, stir-fry shrimp until just pink, about 2 minutes; set aside.
Stir-fry garlic, ginger, sliced mushrooms, and julienned carrot for 2 minutes.
Add bok choy, stir-fry 1 minute until wilted.
Add noodles, soy sauce, and oyster sauce; toss everything together.
Return shrimp, toss to combine, and serve.
tips: Cook shrimp separately and add back at the end to avoid overcooking.
Lo mein noodles should be saucy but not soupy.

name: Chinese Broccoli with Oyster Sauce
description: Blanched gai lan drizzled with a glossy oyster sauce and garlic oil.
instructions: Trim tough ends of gai lan, cut into 10cm lengths.
Blanch in boiling salted water for 2-3 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp; drain.
Arrange on a serving plate.
Mix oyster sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and 1 tbsp water.
Heat oil, fry sliced garlic until golden, pour garlic oil and sauce over the broccoli.
Drizzle sesame oil and serve.
tips: Do not overcook — gai lan should be bright green and slightly crunchy.
Fried garlic chips add crunch and flavor.

name: Congee
description: Silky Chinese rice porridge slow-cooked until creamy, served with savory toppings.
instructions: Rinse rice and add to a large pot with chicken stock and sliced ginger.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
Cook uncovered for 45-60 minutes, stirring occasionally, until rice breaks down into a creamy porridge.
Season with soy sauce, white pepper, and sesame oil.
Ladle into bowls, top with sliced spring onion, fried shallots, and optional century egg.
tips: Stir occasionally to prevent sticking, especially near the end.
For a silkier texture, soak rice for 30 minutes before cooking.

name: Lion's Head Meatballs
description: Large, tender pork meatballs braised in a napa cabbage and soy broth.
instructions: Mix pork with egg, ginger, 1 tbsp soy sauce, wine, cornstarch, white pepper, and minced spring onion.
Shape into 4 large meatballs (about the size of a tennis ball).
Brown meatballs in oil on all sides; do not cook through.
Line a clay pot or Dutch oven with napa cabbage leaves.
Nestle meatballs in the cabbage, pour in stock and remaining soy sauce.
Cover and braise on low heat for 25-30 minutes until meatballs are cooked through and cabbage is silky.
tips: The meatballs should be large — lion's head size, not regular meatballs.
Braising in cabbage keeps the meatballs moist and flavors the broth.

name: Cumin Lamb
description: Xinjiang-style stir-fried lamb with cumin seeds, dried chilies, and onions.
instructions: Slice lamb thinly, marinate with soy sauce and cornstarch for 15 minutes.
Toast cumin seeds in a dry wok until fragrant; set aside half.
Heat oil in wok until smoking, stir-fry lamb in batches until browned.
Add sliced onion, garlic, dried chilies, and chili flakes; stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add remaining toasted cumin seeds and salt, toss 1 minute.
Garnish with cilantro and serve with flatbread or rice.
tips: Toast cumin seeds until fragrant but not burnt — they go from perfect to bitter quickly.
Use boneless lamb leg for the best texture.

name: Egg Drop Soup
description: Light chicken broth thickened with cornstarch and finished with silky egg ribbons.
instructions: Bring chicken stock to a boil, season with soy sauce, salt, and white pepper.
Mix cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold water, stir into the boiling broth to thicken slightly.
Beat eggs. Reduce heat so broth is barely simmering.
Slowly drizzle beaten egg into the broth in a thin stream while stirring gently.
Turn off heat, drizzle sesame oil, and garnish with sliced spring onion.
tips: Pour egg in a very thin stream for delicate ribbons, not clumps.
The broth should be just simmering, not boiling vigorously, when adding eggs.

name: Scallion Pancakes
description: Flaky, crispy Chinese flatbread layered with sesame oil and chopped scallions.
instructions: Mix flour with boiling water, knead until smooth, rest 30 minutes covered.
Divide into 4 portions. Roll each into a thin circle.
Brush with sesame oil, sprinkle salt and chopped spring onion.
Roll up into a log, then coil into a spiral. Flatten and roll into a circle again.
Pan-fry in oil over medium heat for 2-3 minutes per side until golden and flaky.
Serve with a dipping sauce of soy sauce, vinegar, and chili oil.
tips: Boiling water makes the dough softer and more pliable than cold water dough.
The rolling-coiling-rolling technique creates the flaky layers.

name: Chicken Katsu
description: Crispy panko-breaded chicken cutlet served with tonkatsu sauce, shredded cabbage, and rice.
instructions: Pound chicken breasts to 1.5cm even thickness. Season with salt and pepper.
Set up breading station: flour, beaten eggs, panko breadcrumbs.
Coat chicken in flour, dip in egg, press into panko, ensuring full coverage.
Shallow fry in oil at 170C for 4-5 minutes per side until deep golden.
Drain on a wire rack. Rest 3 minutes, then slice into 2cm strips.
Serve with shredded cabbage, tonkatsu sauce, lemon wedge, and steamed rice.
tips: Press panko firmly onto the chicken for a thick, crunchy crust.
Shallow frying works perfectly — no need to deep fry.

name: Gyudon (Beef Bowl)
description: Thinly sliced beef simmered with onions in a sweet soy-dashi broth, served over rice.
instructions: Slice onion into thin half-moons.
Combine dashi, soy sauce, mirin, sugar, sake, and ginger in a pan; bring to a simmer.
Add onion, cook 5 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add beef slices, spread out in a single layer, cook 3-4 minutes.
Optionally crack eggs over the top, cover 2 minutes for soft-set eggs.
Serve over hot rice, garnished with pickled ginger and spring onion.
tips: Slice beef as thin as possible — partially freeze it for easier slicing.
The broth should just coat the rice, not make it soupy.

name: Yakitori
description: Japanese grilled chicken skewers glazed with a sweet soy tare sauce.
instructions: Cut chicken into 3cm pieces and spring onion into 3cm lengths.
Make tare sauce: simmer soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar until reduced by half and syrupy.
Thread chicken and spring onion alternately onto skewers.
Grill over high heat, turning frequently, for 8-10 minutes.
Brush with tare sauce in the last 2 minutes, allowing it to caramelize.
Sprinkle shichimi togarashi and serve immediately.
tips: Baste with tare at the end only — too early and the sugar burns.
Chicken thigh is essential — breast dries out over high heat.

name: Okonomiyaki
description: Japanese savory cabbage pancake with pork, topped with okonomiyaki sauce, mayo, and bonito flakes.
instructions: Shred cabbage finely. Mix flour, eggs, and dashi into a batter.
Fold in cabbage, tenkasu, and pickled ginger gently — do not overmix.
Heat oil in a large pan. Pour batter into a round shape about 2cm thick.
Lay pork belly slices on top. Cook on medium 5 minutes until bottom is golden.
Flip carefully (use a plate to help). Cook pork side down 5 more minutes.
Top with okonomiyaki sauce, mayo in zigzag lines, bonito flakes, and aonori.
tips: Do not press down on the pancake while cooking — it should be thick and fluffy.
The bonito flakes dance in the heat — that is normal and part of the presentation.

name: Tonkotsu Ramen
description: Rich, creamy pork bone broth ramen with chashu pork, soft-boiled egg, and nori.
instructions: Heat tonkotsu broth with soy sauce, mirin, minced garlic, and ginger. Simmer 10 minutes.
Cook ramen noodles according to package directions. Drain.
Slice chashu pork, warm briefly in the simmering broth.
Halve the soft-boiled eggs (yolks should be jammy, not hard).
Divide noodles into bowls, ladle hot broth over.
Top with chashu, egg halves, corn, sliced spring onion, nori, and sesame seeds.
tips: The broth should be milky white and rich — this comes from long boiling of pork bones.
A jammy egg (6.5 minutes boiled, then ice bath) is the ramen standard.

name: Tempura
description: Light, crispy battered shrimp and vegetables fried to golden perfection, served with tentsuyu dipping sauce.
instructions: Make tentsuyu: combine dashi, soy sauce, and mirin; warm gently.
Mix flour and cornstarch. Beat egg with ice water. Combine with flour — mix briefly, leaving lumps.
Heat oil to 180C. Peel and devein shrimp, score belly to prevent curling.
Slice vegetables into thin pieces for even cooking.
Dip in batter lightly, fry in batches: vegetables 2-3 minutes, shrimp 2 minutes until pale golden.
Serve immediately with tentsuyu and grated daikon.
tips: Ice-cold batter and lumps are essential — overmixed batter becomes heavy and doughy.
Fry in small batches to maintain oil temperature.

name: Udon Noodle Soup
description: Thick wheat noodles in a hot dashi-soy broth topped with tempura bits, spring onion, and kamaboko.
instructions: Bring dashi stock to a simmer with soy sauce and mirin.
Cook udon noodles according to package (boil for frozen, rinse for fresh). Drain.
Rehydrate wakame in warm water for 5 minutes if using dried.
Divide noodles into bowls, ladle hot broth over.
Top with kamaboko slices, tenkasu, wakame, and sliced spring onion.
Sprinkle shichimi togarashi to taste and serve hot.
tips: Do not overcook udon — they should be chewy, not mushy.
The broth should be light and clear, not heavy.

name: Miso Glazed Salmon
description: Salmon fillets marinated in sweet white miso glaze, broiled until caramelized.
instructions: Mix miso, mirin, sake, sugar, and soy sauce into a smooth marinade.
Coat salmon fillets with marinade, cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (up to 24 hours).
Wipe off excess marinade (it will burn under the broiler).
Broil salmon on high for 6-8 minutes until the top is deeply caramelized and fish is cooked through.
Drizzle sesame oil, garnish with spring onion and sesame seeds.
Serve with steamed rice and pickled vegetables.
tips: Wipe off excess marinade before broiling to prevent burning.
